title: Murderoni

jokes.text: Welcome to entertainment and earth invasion tonite!
jokes.punchline: Show title combines entertainment news with alien invasion plans
jokes.explanation: Classic Morbo character trait - casual mixing of news with conquest plans

jokes.text: We'll be talking with general extermulo, Who will crush the human race under his boot Unless his demands are met immediately! But first, celebrity buzz.
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivoting to trivial celebrity news
jokes.explanation: Perfect satire of news priorities - existential threat takes backseat to gossip

jokes.text: loyal fans have turned out By the several to pay tribute
jokes.setup: Expected phrase 'by the hundreds' or 'by the thousands'
jokes.punchline: 'By the several' - pathetically small number
jokes.explanation: Clever subversion of expected crowd size description

jokes.text: evidently forced into servitude Aboard a crab fishing boat. So he's, like, legally dead now. Want to get married and whatever?
jokes.setup: Dramatic soap opera revelation about husband's fate
jokes.punchline: Casual marriage proposal with 'whatever'
jokes.explanation: Perfect parody of soap opera melodrama mixed with millennial casualness

jokes.text: I'm... Pregnant. And you're not the father! Okeydoke.
jokes.punchline: Completely underwhelmed 'Okeydoke' response
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - dramatic buildup demolished by casual response

jokes.text: He would've acted so hard, The camera would've broken down. In tears!
jokes.punchline: Acting so intense it would physically damage equipment and make camera cry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from good acting to physically impossible emotional impact

jokes.text: That's what bath time is for.
jokes.setup: Leela suggests talking to each other more
jokes.punchline: Bender has designated talking time in the bath
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - robots bathing and having conversation schedules

jokes.text: If we talk to each other any more than that, It'll ruin our friendship.
jokes.punchline: Bender believes communication destroys friendship
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender anti-logic about relationships, playing into his selfish personality.

jokes.text: This must be linda: A difficult time for people who confuse tv actors with actual friends.
jokes.punchline: Direct commentary on parasocial relationships
jokes.explanation: Sharp meta-commentary on fandom and media relationships

jokes.text: Here's... Corpsey!
jokes.punchline: Presenting corpse like a game show prize
jokes.explanation: Dark humor perfectly delivered with Bender's cheerful presentation

jokes.text: That doesn't sound very scientific. Not to the layman, no. But that's how it works!
jokes.setup: Professor explains need to deal with Robot Devil
jokes.punchline: Professor admits it's unscientific but insists it's correct
jokes.explanation: Classic Professor - dismissing concerns with pseudo-authority

jokes.text: will you shut up?! You've been talking for a solid year. You know, hell used to be a nice place before you got here.
jokes.setup: Calculon doing dramatic monologue in hell
jokes.punchline: Even the devil is annoyed by his overacting
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Calculon's ham acting annoys even Satan

jokes.text: A what? You know, the disembodied software blah blah whatever.
jokes.punchline: Gives up on technical explanation with 'blah blah whatever'
jokes.explanation: Very Bender - starting formal then getting lazy mid-sentence

jokes.text: It's between one and three. Four! Between one and three. Not including one or three. 'm'! Is he right? Yes. The number I was thinking of was the letter 'm.'
jokes.setup: Simple guessing game with clear parameters
jokes.punchline: Escalates to impossible answer that's somehow correct
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist escalation - defying logic while somehow being correct

jokes.text: This is the least scientific thing I've ever seen. You be quiet!
jokes.punchline: Professor getting defensive when called out
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier joke, shows Professor's stubborn denial

jokes.text: In the name of satan. Rise from the dead
jokes.punchline: Innocent Amy speaking Satanic incantations
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - sweet Amy doing demonic ritual

jokes.text: Seriously, this could not seem less scientific.
jokes.punchline: Leela's repeated incredulity
jokes.explanation: Third iteration of same joke - getting stale but maintains character consistency

jokes.text: Wherefore... Verily... No autographs, please.
jokes.punchline: First coherent words are refusing autographs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon moment - even while disoriented, his ego shows through

jokes.text: Won't leave my trailer for less than $40,000.
jokes.punchline: Making salary demands while barely conscious
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Calculon's priorities even when addled

jokes.text: Who is she? Don't worry about it. Not important.
jokes.punchline: Bender's dismissive response to negative review
jokes.explanation: Decent friendship moment - Bender protecting Calculon from criticism

jokes.text: But the twist is, in this show, the singers judge the judges. It's got attitude.
jokes.setup: TV executive describing new show concept
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous reality show twist with proud 'attitude' claim
jokes.explanation: Decent satire of reality TV's desperate twists and buzzwords

jokes.text: A hip new demographic: Our network is targeting People who don't like you.
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest about targeting anti-Calculon demographic
jokes.explanation: Sharp media satire - executive's clinical cruelty about audience targeting

jokes.text: And so I am proud to tear up his star and replace it With a second star for the fabulous robert wagner.
jokes.punchline: Immediately replacing memorial with second star for living actor
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Calculon's fall from grace - not even unique star

jokes.text: This is going to be the greatest career resurrection since... Jesus-- aloha from hawaii.
jokes.setup: Expected religious reference to Jesus's resurrection
jokes.punchline: References Elvis's Hawaii comeback special instead
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection - sets up religious reference but delivers showbiz callback

jokes.text: But I reckon, if I had to do it all again, I'd murder those astronauts just the same. Wouldn't you, folks? Wouldn't... You?
jokes.setup: One-man show with folksy, relatable tone
jokes.punchline: Casually admits to murdering astronauts, awkward audience pause
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - casual confession in intimate setting creates perfect awkwardness

jokes.text: 'worst play in history.' 'calculon dies for second time.' 'theater to be demolished to remove stink of new show.' '12 hospitalized for boredom at play.'
jokes.punchline: Reviews escalate to impossible levels of bad
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of bad reviews to absurd extremes - building demolition is inspired

jokes.text: It says nine of them are gonna be okay.
jokes.punchline: Optimistic focus on survivors as if it were physical trauma
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry optimism - treating boredom hospitalization like actual medical emergency

jokes.text: Ye gods! What manner of beef-witted popinjay am I?
jokes.punchline: Even self-deprecation is overwrought with archaic insults
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon - even self-criticism is theatrical with 'beef-witted popinjay'

jokes.text: And not the kind you hope for When you're stranded on a desert island.
jokes.punchline: Clarifies he's not the useful survival kind of ham
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay on 'ham' - takes metaphor literally with survival scenario

jokes.text: Calculon? Yes? Are you done talking, or is this a long pause? I'm done talking. Okay, that's what I thought.
jokes.punchline: Fry can't tell if it's depression or signature pause
jokes.explanation: Perfect meta-joke about Calculon's signature move - uncertainty creates awkward comedy

jokes.text: Your voice is so annoying! You always leave the toilet seat crushed!
jokes.setup: Demonstrating how they argue when forced to talk
jokes.punchline: Robot-specific complaint about crushing toilet seat
jokes.explanation: Good robot-specific humor - normal complaint twisted by Bender's strength

jokes.text: That was the first time I ever believed You were feeling a genuine emotion. I was. It was shame.
jokes.punchline: Calculon matter-of-factly identifies the new emotion as shame
jokes.explanation: Nice character growth moment with dry humor - Calculon's clinical self-awareness

jokes.text: No, leela. In the world of theater There are no second acts.
jokes.setup: Leela suggesting he can make a comeback
jokes.punchline: Misapplies theater structure metaphor literally
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on theatrical structure vs. life - though somewhat forced

jokes.text: Where's calculon? And who's this goomer? It's me, calculon.
jokes.punchline: Disguise so bad he's unrecognizable as generic 'goomer'
jokes.explanation: Standard disguise joke but 'goomer' is a nice specific insult

jokes.text: Mr. Ben derisgreat?
jokes.punchline: Name is obvious pun on 'been there, is great'
jokes.explanation: Weak pun name - too obvious and not particularly clever

jokes.text: Old, past your prime, a relic from a lamer era. You really do look disgusting. A real calculon type. Just blech!
jokes.punchline: Unknowingly insulting actual Calculon to his face
jokes.explanation: Good ironic situation with escalating insults, though somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Better yet, I'll whip their emotions into a frenzy By taking a dramatic... / Actors to the set!
jokes.setup: Calculon about to do his signature dramatic pause
jokes.punchline: Director interrupts before he can complete it
jokes.explanation: Great timing joke - the pause is interrupted before it can happen, which is the opposite of what Calculon expects.

jokes.text: This show is about me! And it always has been! Now, find the co-executive hack who wrote this drivel And tell him I want 37 more climactic speeches By lunch!
jokes.punchline: Demanding absurd number of climactic speeches by arbitrary deadline
jokes.explanation: Perfect Calculon diva moment - specific ridiculous demands show his ego perfectly

jokes.text: I've never heard that phrase-- what does that mean?
jokes.punchline: Calculon has never had to do a second take
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - shows Calculon's past success and current naivety

jokes.text: My humble ranks among the greatest In the history of the universe!
jokes.setup: Being told to act more humble
jokes.punchline: Boasts about quality of his humility
jokes.explanation: Excellent paradoxical joke - bragging about humility perfectly captures Calculon's ego problem

jokes.text: My humble ranks among the greatest In the history of the universe!
jokes.punchline: Boasting that his humility is the greatest ever
jokes.explanation: Perfect contradiction - being arrogant about being humble. Quintessential Calculon logic delivered with complete sincerity.

jokes.text: You're a grade-b actor who died And was immediately forgotten. You're nothing but a pompous windbag Who lost touch with genuine emotion years ago. The sad fact is, we should have left you dead, Because you can't possibly do a scene like this.
jokes.setup: Leela trying to motivate Calculon through reverse psychology
jokes.punchline: Brutal honesty delivered to help him access real emotion
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment - Leela's tough love approach sets up emotional breakthrough

jokes.text: Maybe even a little bit better.
jokes.setup: Calculon saying world got on fine without him
jokes.punchline: Quietly devastating admission that his absence improved things
jokes.explanation: Powerful understated moment - small line with big emotional impact

jokes.text: I thought I could just step back into my old life. But it seems the world got on fine without me. Maybe even a little bit better.
jokes.punchline: Heartbreaking self-awareness delivered with real feeling
jokes.explanation: Powerful character moment - Calculon's first genuine performance comes from real pain. The comedy comes from the context, not the delivery.

jokes.text: I was programmed to take credit for this.
jokes.setup: Being asked to take a bow for good performance
jokes.punchline: Admits his ego is literally programmed behavior
jokes.explanation: Clever robot joke - makes character trait literal programming

jokes.text: Very well. Just one. / Whew, that was close.
jokes.setup: Calculon agreeing to take 'just one' bow
jokes.punchline: Bender relieved he stopped at one, implying he might have continued forever
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Bender knows Calculon well enough to fear he'd bow indefinitely. Shows their friendship.

jokes.text: Whew, that was close.
jokes.setup: Almost taking a bow but stopping himself
jokes.punchline: Treating restraint from ego as narrow escape
jokes.explanation: Nice button on character growth - ego still there but now self-aware

jokes.text: Shut up! I'm watching the thing!
jokes.punchline: Bender prioritizing TV over conversation as established
jokes.explanation: Good callback to friendship dynamic - things back to normal

jokes.text: Haven't they suffered enough?!
jokes.setup: Calculon offering to perform one-man show in Hell
jokes.punchline: Even Satan thinks that's too cruel for the damned
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback and escalation - if Satan thinks it's too cruel, it must be truly awful

tmdb_overview: A conspiracy theory makes shocking claims about the basement of the local pizzeria.
